title: analytics — HTTP middleware
description: One owned middleware — `analytics.enforce_consent` — used on funnel + experiment report routes to refuse queries that would return data belonging to subjects who withdrew consent (the query itself is not sensitive, but composing per-subject rollups from suppressed events is).

middleware.purpose: Refuses report queries (funnel + experiment) when the caller lacks the `analytics.data-viewer` permission OR when the query would include subjects who have withdrawn `analytics` consent. Withdrawn-consent subjects are transparently EXCLUDED from the report; when exclusion would drop >20% of the sample, returns HTTP 422 with a warning so the caller doesn't misinterpret partial data.
steps: Load caller's permissions from Sanctum abilities.
For the report route: probe the underlying event set to determine consent-suppression ratio.
Attach a `X-Analytics-Consent-Suppressed-Ratio: <float>` header to the response envelope so the caller can render a warning banner.
When ratio > 0.20 AND caller lacks analytics.data-viewer.override, return 422 NOTIFICATIONS_ANALYTICS_HIGH_CONSENT_SUPPRESSION.

entitlements.check.reason: Universal entitlement gate. Applied on every route via `entitlements.check:analytics_capture` etc.
